content:
9 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

My day starts like most junior devs: with the eternal struggle between “I should be productive” and “just five more minutes.” I finally roll out of bed at 7:15, grab coffee, and scroll through dev Twitter while half-awake. Someone’s arguing about Tailwind vs. CSS-in-JS again. I bookmark it for later — not because I care, but because I know it’ll come up in standup.
8:30 AM — The Commute (Or The Walk to My Desk)
I work hybrid, so today I’m remote. I “commute” from my bedroom to my desk, which takes approximately 12 seconds. I open my laptop, and the ritual begins:

• Slack — 47 unread messages. Most are from overnight, some are memes, a few are actually important.

• GitHub — 3 PR reviews assigned to me. My heart rate spikes slightly.

• Jira — The ticket I was “almost done with” yesterday has 2 new comments from the PM. Great.
I spend the first 20 minutes just catching up. No code written yet. Already tired.
9:00 AM — Daily Standup (AKA “What Did I Actually Do Yesterday?”)
We hop on Zoom. The senior devs talk about architectural decisions and microservices. When it’s my turn, I say something…
